notes: SHIP v1.14.274 - PHASE-SHEET BACK-TRAP FIX (owner un-parked: ".273 verified, ship the back-trap fix"). ONE registry entry. The .268 phase dock opens #ph-sheet (class 'open') over the rack detail, but nav_back()'s sheet-awareness registry (Cut 13, :17358) - which exists precisely to close the topmost open sheet and STOP before popping nav - never listed it. So ONE back press OVER-TRAVELLED: it closed the sheet AND popped nav off the rack detail (reproduced live this session, and originally surfaced by web-Claude's HANDOFF-PHASE-SHEET-BACK; it is my own .268 miss - I registered the dock's dismissal in showMode but not in nav_back). FIX: add { id:'ph-sheet', cls:'open', close: phdock_close } as the FIRST registry entry (highest z: #ph-sheet is z-150 over the deepest surface). phdock_close is the rule-7-safe closer (restores body.overflow only if it took the lock), and the registry only invokes close() when the element actually carries the class, so it is a no-op when the sheet is shut. ==== VERIFIED LIVE (real app, realistic stack deploy-detail -> rack-detail -> phase sheet): one back press -> sheet closed, STILL on the rack detail (ph-dock-on true), body.overflow restored. Before the fix that same press also left the rack detail. Regressions all pass: a SECOND back then navigates normally (leaves the rack detail); back with NO sheet open navigates in ONE press (not eaten); scrim-tap and X still close (they call phdock_close directly, untouched by the registry). ?legacy=1 proven clean: #ph-sheet exists in markup but can NEVER get .open (phdock_open/render are rd-only, the dock is body.rd.ops-detail.ph-dock-on gated), so the new entry can never match - a pure no-op; legacy nav_back behaviour byte-identical. ==== RACK SCENE untouched (the re-armed lock holds): this is nav/DOM plumbing, zero lines near rackElevation_render3D/materials/lights/camera/floor. Gates: node --check 4/0; CSS 12 blocks balanced; CRLF uniform; three-stamp .273->.274. John iPhone gate: [1] open the phase dock sheet on a rack, press the phone/in-app BACK once -> the sheet closes and you STAY on the rack detail (you do NOT get kicked back to the deploy list). [2] press back again -> now it navigates normally. [3] on a rack with no sheet open, back navigates in one press as before. [4] drag-down / X / scrim-tap still dismiss the sheet. [5] ?legacy=1 unchanged."
